subject: Re: Not-a-Number value error in WarpTools
body:
The data is TIFF, so not needing to be reciprocal (simple .gain to .mrc
conversion suffices). I believe for EER you would still need to take the
reciprocal as before.

On Thu, 8 Aug 2024, 13:21 3P1L, <philipp.stawski@gmail.com> wrote:

> *Hi Shintaro!*
>
> This is EER data, right? Did you also take the reciprocal of the gain?
>
> In previous versions this was always required if I'm not mistaken ...
>
> While WARP (1.x) did not complain if you didn't use the 1/gain, the
> results of course would be bad...
>
>
> *So also as potential question for the WARP team*: do we still need to
> manually convert the gain in the same way?
>
>
> If so, I could see that *1/gain vs *gain could cause problems with some
> gain files ...
> On Thursday, August 8, 2024 at 12:50:23 PM UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Shintaro,
>>
>> Good to see you here! Would you mind opening a GitHub issue with a link
>> to download the problematic gain and a movie?
>>
>> I’ll take a look but it won’t be for at least a couple of weeks as I’m
>> just about to start vacation - maybe Dimitry will take a look before 🙂
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Aug 8, 2024, at 11:23, Shintaro Aibara <shintar...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>>
>> Hi Community!
>>
>> A bit of an odd one, but perhaps I could be pointed in the right
>> direction....
>>
>> I encountered an error where WarpTools crashes on the first step,
>> specifically with a particular gain reference. This gain reference was
>> collected as a .gain format, converted to a mrc file with tif2mrc.
>>
>> Presumably something is wrong with this particular file as I get a
>> System.ArtithmeticException error with this file. Changing with a gain from
>> another day doesn't raise this error.
>>
>> This is an issue specifically with WarpTools as Warp 1.1-beta can handle
>> this file fine. Looking as the image with --stats on relion image handler
>> doest raise any flags with me.
>>
>> Best wishes
>> Shintaro
>>
>> --
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
